Details: Removal of strychnine from water by GAC is generally effective (77 percent [1489] to greater than 86 percent removal - this percent removal was limited by the detection limit for the contaminant[618]). One GAC unit tested was a component of a point-of-use drinking water treatment system consisting of a RO module, a UV+ozone module, and the GAC filter [1489]. The other GAC unit tested was a post-membrane carbon filter part of a point-of-entry drinking water treatment system [618]. The percent removal range is for GAC alone. Both studies showed that the removal of strychnine at greater than 99 percent can be achieved by the RO+GAC and UV/ozone advanced simultaneous oxidation+GAC treatment combinations. GAC properties were not reported.
